response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior developer with broad full-stack experience and notable strengths in D-language low-latency systems, GIS platforms, and C/C++ exposure at National Instruments. However, the role demands modern C++ (or Rust) as primary, and the resume shows only early-career C/C++ work (2007-2008) with no recent commercial C++ or Rust experience, and no direct routing/tiling or distributed-pipeline background. GIS founding work and Sociomantic low-latency experience partially offset the gap, making this a borderline maybe pending a technical screen on modern C++.
strengths: Long-running GIS platform founder with geospatial data processing depth
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing, Linux
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust experience in last ~17 years; primary stack is TS/Node/D
No direct routing, tiling, or large-scale data pipeline experience
Lacks distributed-systems fundamentals (fault tolerance, consistency) called out in JD
